hitchens_question: When the Pentagon announces a "Cyber Mastery Incentive Pay" program on the same day that Russian satellite jamming cripples GPS systems and Chinese websites target American security personnel, are we witnessing the birth of a new kind of warfare, or merely the final admission that the old kind never ended—and that our entire conception of "peacetime" has been a comforting delusion? How convenient it must be for defense contractors and military bureaucrats alike to discover that the enemy has thoughtfully provided a threat so invisible, so continuous, and so perfectly suited to justifying unlimited budgets that it makes the military-industrial complex look like a model of restraint by comparison.
